close_reason: Cancel update + orphan GC with pg advisory locks — implemented, tested (3 E2E + 3 unit), pushed
description: POST .../update/{uid}/cancel: Cancel active update, release stack lock, mark update canceled. Auth via update-token. Background GC worker: scan every 60s for active updates where now() > lease_expires_at + lease_duration. Auto-cancel orphaned updates, clear Stack.CurrentOperation. Also run reconciliation at server startup. internal/updates/gc_worker.go
title: Cancel update + orphan GC worker

close_reason: Phase 1 complete: GET /api/user, GET /api/capabilities, auth middleware — pulumi login succeeds
description: Implement GET /api/user and GET /api/capabilities so that 'pulumi login http://localhost:8080' succeeds. Auth via Descope Go SDK.
title: Phase 1: Login-able Server

SPA embedded in Go binary via go:embed, served via SPA handler with client-side routing fallback. Multi-stage Docker build. Stateless architecture preserved — UI is static assets, all data via existing REST API.
description: React web UI differentiating Strata from headless alternatives (e.g., hatemosphere/pulumi-backend). Stack list view, update timeline, live log streaming during active updates. Event retrieval API with continuationToken pagination. Maintains the same stateless architecture — UI served as static assets, all data fetched via the existing API. Vite + React + TailwindCSS.

close_reason: 15 E2E tests pass: health, capabilities, login, whoami, stack init/dup/list/select/rm/lifecycle, rename/nonexistent/conflict. All via real Pulumi CLI against live server.
description: Acceptance test: pulumi stack init test-org/test-project/dev (exit 0), pulumi stack ls --json (shows dev), pulumi stack rm dev --yes (exit 0, stack gone). Verify HEAD project exists returns 200 during, 404 after.
title: E2E test: pulumi stack init/ls/rm

close_reason: No separate AuthZ middleware needed. Descope JWT carries tenant roles. The existing auth middleware just adds a role check when extracting Caller from token/JWT — not a separate layer.
description: Simplified after Descope research. Create internal/http/middleware/authz.go. Runs AFTER AuthN middleware. The Caller struct (populated by either TokenAuthenticator or DescopeAuthenticator) carries OrgMemberships []OrgRole. Middleware: (1) Extract org from URL path for /api/stacks/{org}/... routes. (2) Call caller.HasOrgRole(org, minRole) — a method on the Caller struct, not a service. (3) Route→role mapping: GET → viewer, POST/PATCH (update, checkpoint, destroy, import) → member, DELETE stack/rename → admin. (4) For /api/user/stacks, handler filters to caller's org memberships. (5) Return 403 with JSON error on denied. The HasOrgRole helper + OrgRole type live in internal/auth/service.go alongside Caller.
title: AuthZ middleware — enforce permissions on all API routes

close_reason: GET /api/user returns authenticated user with githubLogin, organizations array, identities. CallerFromContext/ContextWithCaller helpers added. Build clean.
description: Return authenticated user info. CRITICAL: response MUST include non-empty githubLogin (CLI hard-crashes without it), organizations array where each org has githubLogin, optional tokenInfo. Map Descope user fields to this shape. Full shape: {id, githubLogin, name, email, avatarUrl, organizations: [{githubLogin, name, avatarUrl}], identities, tokenInfo: {name, organization, team}}. Handler: internal/http/handlers/user_handler.go
title: GET /api/user endpoint

close_reason: VERIFIED: pulumi login http://localhost:8080 succeeds. Output: 'Logged in to http://localhost:8080 as dev-user'. GET /api/capabilities returns {capabilities:[]}, GET /api/user returns correct shape with githubLogin, 401 without auth.
description: Acceptance test: start Strata server, run 'pulumi login http://localhost:8080' with valid token. Assert: exit code 0, output contains 'Logged in to'. Also verify: curl /api/capabilities returns {capabilities:[]}, curl /api/user returns valid shape with githubLogin. Test in test/e2e/.
title: E2E test: pulumi login succeeds

Implement POST endpoints for all 4 kinds: /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/update, .../preview, .../refresh, .../destroy. Accept UpdateProgramRequest, return UpdateProgramResponse with updateID. Set Stack.CurrentOperation. Return RequiredPolicies as empty array (not null).

close_reason: PulumiAccept + CORS middleware added. Middleware chain: RequestID→Logging→Recovery→CORS→PulumiAccept. go build + go vet clean.
description: Chi router with middleware stack: request ID, structured logging (slog), panic recovery, CORS. Custom middleware: Accept header validation (must accept application/vnd.pulumi+8), unified error response encoder (apitype.ErrorResponse format: {code, message}). Wire to app lifecycle with graceful shutdown.
title: HTTP server + middleware foundation

close_reason: OrgAuth middleware enforces org membership on all /api/stacks/{org} routes, ListStacks checks caller membership
description: Audit every SQL query in the codebase to ensure tenant isolation. Key areas: (1) GET /api/user/stacks currently returns ALL stacks. Must filter to only orgs where the caller is a member. Modify the ListStacks query to JOIN organization_members. (2) The stack auto-create flow (CreateStack → INSERT org ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) must check that the caller is a member of the target org, not just auto-create orgs for anyone. (3) findStackID has no tenant check — any authenticated user can resolve any stack UUID. After AuthZ middleware is in place, this is blocked at the middleware level, but defense-in-depth says the service layer should also verify. (4) Export/Import endpoints: verify caller has access to the target stack's org. Produce a checklist of every endpoint and confirm AuthZ coverage.
title: Tenant isolation audit — verify all queries respect org boundaries

close_reason: Encrypt/decrypt endpoints implemented: AES-256-GCM with HKDF per-stack key derivation, dev mode auto-key, 6 crypto unit tests + 6 handler unit tests + 3 E2E tests passing.
description: POST /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/encrypt and .../decrypt: Implement server-side AES-256-GCM encryption for Pulumi secrets. Accept EncryptValueRequest/DecryptValueRequest, return EncryptValueResponse/DecryptValueResponse. Required for 'pulumi config set --secret'. Without these, users must use --secrets-provider=passphrase. Also stub batch-encrypt/batch-decrypt (Tier 2) returning 404 initially.
title: Encrypt/decrypt endpoints

close_reason: No longer needed. Dev mode keeps a thin DevAuthenticator mock. Prod uses Descope for everything — no DB bootstrap of users/tokens required.
description: In dev mode, on server startup: (1) Upsert a user from STRATA_DEV_USER_LOGIN into the users table. (2) Upsert an org from STRATA_DEV_ORG_LOGIN into organizations. (3) Upsert org membership (admin role) into organization_members. (4) Upsert an api_token: hash STRATA_DEV_AUTH_TOKEN with SHA-256, store in api_tokens with token_prefix for display. All upserts via INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so it's idempotent across restarts. This replaces the current pattern where DevAuthenticator hardcodes a Caller from config — after bootstrap, dev mode uses the exact same DB-backed token validation as production. Place in internal/auth/bootstrap.go. Log the token prefix on startup for discoverability.
title: Dev bootstrap — seed user, org, membership, token on startup

close_reason: Descope console/widgets manage org membership in prod. Dev mode uses organization_members table directly. No custom API needed.
description: Add REST endpoints for managing organization membership. (1) GET /api/orgs/{org}/members — list org members with roles. Requires admin or member role. (2) POST /api/orgs/{org}/members — add member: {userLogin, role}. Requires admin role. Lookup user by github_login, insert into organization_members. (3) PATCH /api/orgs/{org}/members/{userLogin} — change role: {role}. Requires admin. Cannot demote the last admin. (4) DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/members/{userLogin} — remove member. Requires admin. Cannot remove the last admin. Create internal/http/handlers/org_handler.go. All endpoints enforce AuthZ via the Authorizer.
title: Org membership API — invite, remove, change roles

close_reason: Descope Console + Management SDK handle access key creation/revocation. No custom token management API needed.
description: Add REST endpoints for managing personal access tokens. (1) POST /api/user/tokens — create a new token: generate 32 random bytes, return the raw token ONCE in response (never stored), store SHA-256 hash + first 8 chars as prefix in api_tokens. Request: {description, expiresAt?}. Response: {id, token, prefix, description, createdAt, expiresAt}. (2) GET /api/user/tokens — list caller's tokens (id, prefix, description, lastUsedAt, expiresAt, createdAt). Never return the raw token. (3) DELETE /api/user/tokens/{id} — soft-revoke by setting revoked_at=now(). Invalidate ttlcache entry. Only the token owner can manage their tokens. Create internal/http/handlers/token_handler.go.
title: Token management API — create, list, revoke PATs

close_reason: Phase 2 complete: all 5 child tasks closed. Stack create/get/delete/list/rename + project exists all work. 24 integration tests pass, E2E smoke test verified with Pulumi CLI.

close_reason: Delta checkpoint support implemented: PATCH checkpointdelta endpoint with gotextdiff-compatible delta apply (no external deps), SHA-256 hash verification, capabilities advertised. 8 delta unit tests + 3 handler unit tests passing.
description: PATCH .../checkpoint/delta: Accept PatchUpdateCheckpointDeltaRequest with textual diff + checkpointHash (SHA256). Apply byte-offset delta to last-persisted checkpoint. Verify hash before and after. Advertise delta-checkpoint-uploads-v2 capability in GET /api/capabilities. Critical for states >1MB where CLI defaults to deltas. Reference: hatemosphere's internal/engine/delta.go for applyDelta logic.
title: Delta checkpoint support

close_reason: Streaming gzip middleware: sync.Pool for reader/writer, request decompression + response compression. Build clean.
description: Handle Content-Encoding: gzip on request bodies (decompress) and Accept-Encoding: gzip on responses (compress). Critical for large state payloads (10-50MB). Streaming implementation — don't buffer entire payload in memory. internal/http/middleware/gzip.go
title: Gzip compression middleware

close_reason: Implemented: 5 Tier 2 endpoints (update history, latest update, stack tags, CLI version, default org). 12 new unit tests. All passing, zero lint issues.
description: Implement remaining Tier 2 endpoints that gracefully 404'd until now: GET .../updates (paginated update history), GET .../updates/latest, POST .../rename, PATCH .../tags, GET /api/cli/version, GET /api/user/organizations/default. These improve UX but CLI survives without them.
title: Tier 2 endpoints: history, tags, rename, version check

close_reason: Descope provides built-in RBAC with per-tenant roles in JWT claims. AuthZ reduces to helper methods on Caller struct, not a separate service.
description: Create internal/authz/service.go with an Authorizer interface. Methods: (1) RequireOrgAccess(caller, orgLogin, minRole) error — check caller.OrgMemberships for the org with at least the given role. Roles: admin > member > viewer. (2) RequireStackAccess(caller, org, project, stack, action) error — resolve action (read/write/admin) to minimum role, then call RequireOrgAccess. Actions: 'read' needs viewer+, 'write' (up/destroy/import) needs member+, 'admin' (delete stack, manage settings) needs admin. (3) Implementation is pure logic on the Caller.OrgMemberships slice — no DB calls (data was loaded at AuthN time). Return sentinel errors: ErrForbidden, ErrNotMember. Keep it simple — org-level RBAC only, no project-level permissions yet.
title: AuthZ service — org membership + role checks

close_reason: LocalStore (atomic write via temp+rename) and S3Store (path-style for MinIO, streaming, NoSuchKey handling) implemented. factory.go wires config to correct backend. go build+vet+tests pass. Subagent added unit tests as bonus.
description: Create internal/storage/blobs/ with interface (Put, Get, Delete, Exists), S3 implementation (aws-sdk-go-v2), local filesystem implementation (for dev). Checkpoints stored as blobs referenced by URI in checkpoints table. Must handle 10-50MB payloads.
title: Blob storage abstraction: S3 + local filesystem

close_reason: Implemented: 6 E2E tests (preview, up, up+destroy, refresh, up with outputs, conflict detection). All 21 E2E tests pass.
description: Full lifecycle acceptance test with a minimal Pulumi program. pulumi up --yes (resources created, state persisted), pulumi preview (no changes), pulumi refresh --yes (state refreshed), pulumi destroy --yes (resources destroyed). Verify checkpoint exists after up, cleared after destroy.
title: E2E test: pulumi up/preview/refresh/destroy

close_reason: Implemented: PATCH checkpoint (standard + verbatim) with sequence number idempotency
description: PATCH /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/update/{uid}/checkpoint: Accept both PatchUpdateCheckpointRequest (version + deployment) and PatchUpdateVerbatimCheckpointRequest (preserves exact JSON, includes sequenceNumber). Distinguish by inspecting request body shape. Write checkpoint blob to S3/filesystem, metadata to Postgres checkpoints table. Enforce monotonic sequenceNumber, ignore duplicate/lower with success no-op (idempotency). Increment stack version. Auth via update-token.
title: Checkpoint PATCH: standard + verbatim

close_reason: Phase 4 complete: state export/import + encrypt/decrypt endpoints. All unit tests (12 crypto + 10 handler) and E2E tests (29 total, 8 new) passing.
description: Export/import state, encrypt/decrypt endpoints, versioned state history. Milestone: pulumi stack export/import + pulumi config set --secret work.
title: Phase 4: State Operations

close_reason: Phase 5 complete: cancel/GC (strata-04y), delta checkpoints (strata-axb), idempotency (strata-wk2), Tier 2 endpoints (strata-b5o). All child tasks closed. Production-viable multi-node deployment achieved.
description: Self-hosted Pulumi backend targeting production-viable horizontal scalability. Architecture: stateless HTTP controllers (no in-memory caches, no local-only state), all mutable state in PostgreSQL, pg advisory locks for background workers, transactions for critical sections, unique indexes to prevent race conditions. Supports clustering out of the box — run N replicas behind a load balancer with shared Postgres + S3 blob storage. Phase 5 delivers: cancel updates, orphan GC (pg advisory locks), delta checkpoints (bandwidth optimization for large states >1MB), idempotency framework (retry-safe mutations via request dedup), and Tier 2 endpoints (update history, tags, CLI version check). Milestone: production-viable multi-node deployment.
title: Phase 5: Resilience

close_reason: Project skeleton complete: all packages created, go build ./... and go vet ./... pass with Go 1.25.7. Fixed: mise.toml pinned to go=1.25.7 to match GOROOT. apitype.ErrorResponse wired in encode package.
description: Create directory structure: cmd/strata/main.go, internal/{app,config,http,auth,stacks,updates,checkpoints,events,crypto,storage,db}. Add core deps: chi/v5, pgx/v5, golang-migrate/v4, google/uuid, pulumi/pulumi apitype. Wire up basic main.go with config loading and graceful shutdown.
title: Project skeleton: cmd/strata/main.go, internal/ layout, go deps

close_reason: Implemented: StartUpdate handler with lease token generation (crypto/rand), SHA-256 hashed storage, 300s default lease
description: POST /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/update/{uid}: Lock the stack, issue lease token (update-token auth scheme), return current deployment + version + TokenExpiration (UNIX timestamp). Must reject if active update exists (populate CurrentOperation in error). Return JournalVersion: 0 to disable journaling. Default lease: 300s. Store lease_token_hash, lease_expires_at in updates table.
title: StartUpdate + lease token issuance

close_reason: docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile, Makefile, .env.example created. Verified: compose config passes, docker build succeeds, postgres+minio come up healthy with proper health checks, minio-init creates bucket. Zero cloud deps — all self-hosted.
description: MOVED TO PHASE 0. Docker Compose stack that serves as both dev environment AND e2e/integration test harness. All services self-contained, no external cloud dependencies. Components: Postgres (metadata+locks), MinIO (S3-compatible blob storage), Strata server (from Dockerfile). Auto-run migrations on startup. Health checks on all services. Compose profiles: 'dev' (server+deps), 'test' (deps only, for running tests against). Makefile targets: make dev, make test-e2e, make down. This is the FIRST thing built — all subsequent work validates against this stack.
title: Docker Compose dev/test environment (foundational)

close_reason: Merged into strata-zso — single Descope authenticator issue covers both JWT and Access Key flows.
description: Create internal/auth/descope.go implementing the Authenticator interface. Uses Descope Go SDK (github.com/descope/go-sdk). (1) On startup, initialize Descope client with STRATA_DESCOPE_PROJECT_ID. (2) ValidateToken: strip 'Bearer ' prefix, call descopeClient.Auth.ValidateSessionWithToken(token), extract user info + tenants claim from the validated token. (3) Map Descope tenants to Caller.OrgMemberships: for each tenant in token.CustomClaims["tenants"], create OrgRole{OrgLogin: tenantID, Role: highestRole}. (4) Wire in main.go: if cfg.AuthMode == "descope", use auth.NewDescopeAuthenticator(cfg). (5) Both Descope and Token authenticators produce the same Caller struct with OrgMemberships populated. PAT auth (TokenAuthenticator) still works in descope mode for CLI — both authenticators can coexist (try Bearer first, fall back to token prefix).
title: Descope authenticator — validate JWTs with tenant roles

close_reason: Implemented cluster profile with Caddy LB + 3 replicas, E2E cluster tests (stack visibility, update lifecycle, cross-instance cancel), STRATA_E2E_URL support, make cluster/e2e-cluster targets
description: Add cluster profile to docker-compose with nginx LB + N strata replicas. Assess TTL cache implications. Add E2E tests exercising multi-instance consistency (create on one instance, read on another). Add Makefile targets (make cluster).
title: Horizontal scaling: Docker Compose replicas with load balancer

close_reason: Implemented: lease renewal (POST renew_lease) and completion (POST complete) with status transitions
description: POST .../renew_lease: Extend lease_expires_at by requested duration (max 300s). Accept duration field. Grace window: 30s skew tolerance. Auth via update-token. POST .../complete: Mark update succeeded/failed, persist final checkpoint, clear Stack.CurrentOperation, release lock. Auth via update-token.
title: Lease renewal + update completion

close_reason: Added 10 E2E multi-tenant tests covering cross-org denial, own-org access, role-based access, list isolation
description: Add E2E tests that verify tenant isolation end-to-end: (1) Create two users (user-a, user-b) in different orgs (org-a, org-b). (2) user-a creates a stack in org-a, runs pulumi up. (3) user-b tries to access org-a's stack — should get 403. (4) user-b creates a stack in org-b — succeeds. (5) GET /api/user/stacks for user-a returns only org-a stacks. (6) Test role escalation: viewer can read but not write, member can write but not delete, admin can do everything. (7) Test token revocation: revoke user-a's token, next request returns 401. Requires the dev bootstrap to support creating multiple users/tokens — may need a test helper or direct DB seeding.
title: E2E tests for multi-tenant AuthZ

close_reason: Stack listing (GET /api/user/stacks with pagination, tag filtering, continuation tokens) and project exists (HEAD /api/stacks/{org}/{project}) fully implemented. Verified via E2E tests.
description: GET /api/user/stacks: paginated stack listing with tag filtering, continuation tokens. HEAD /api/stacks/{org}/{project}: project existence check (DoesProjectExist). Return 200 if exists, 404 if not. Both required for pulumi stack ls/init to work.
title: Stack listing + project exists: GET /api/user/stacks, HEAD /api/stacks/{org}/{project}

close_reason: Implemented: POST events/batch endpoint with EngineEventBatch storage
description: POST /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/update/{uid}/events/batch: Accept EngineEventBatch (array of typed EngineEvents). Store in update_events table with batch_sequence (server-assigned) and event_sequence (from client). Events may arrive out of order — store with sequence numbers, return sorted on retrieval. Auth via update-token.
title: Event ingestion: POST events/batch

close_reason: Stack CRUD fully implemented: POST/GET/DELETE /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}, auto-creates org+project, unique constraint checks, force delete. 24 unit tests + 15 E2E tests all pass.
description: Implement stack create (POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}), get (GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}), delete (DELETE .../{stack}). Delete must return exact string 'Bad Request: Stack still contains resources.' when resources exist. Create auto-creates project if not exists. Store in Postgres stacks+projects tables.
title: Stack CRUD: POST/GET/DELETE /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}

close_reason: Descope Access Keys replace custom token validation. ExchangeAccessKey() returns JWT with tenant/role claims. No need for api_tokens table or custom Token service.
description: Create internal/auth/tokens.go — a TokenService that validates API tokens against the api_tokens table. On each request: hash the provided token with SHA-256, look up token_hash in api_tokens, check not revoked (revoked_at IS NULL) and not expired (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now()), JOIN to users table to build Caller struct with real UserID/GithubLogin/Email, update last_used_at. Return the same auth.Caller struct the current DevAuthenticator returns, but populated from DB. Interface: ValidateAPIToken(ctx, rawToken) (*Caller, error). Add ttlcache for token lookups (30s TTL, invalidate on revoke).
title: User + Token service (DB-backed AuthN)

description: GET /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/export: Return latest checkpoint as UntypedDeployment. GET .../export/{version}: Return specific version. POST /api/stacks/{o}/{p}/{s}/import: Accept UntypedDeployment, create new checkpoint version, return updateId. Must handle large payloads (10-50MB).

close_reason: Implemented: migration 0003 adds checkpoint unique index for ON CONFLICT idempotency. CompleteUpdate and CancelUpdate now idempotent (retry-safe for terminal states). PatchCheckpointVerbatim broken fallback removed. All tests pass, zero lint issues.
description: CLI retries aggressively (1s initial, 2x backoff, 30s max, 4 retries, retryAllMethods on checkpoints). All mutating endpoints must be idempotent. Implement request_dedup table keyed by (scope, idempotency_key). For checkpoint PATCH: use sequenceNumber. For other mutations: derive key from (update_uuid, route, sequence). Return cached response on replay.
title: Idempotency framework for mutating endpoints

close_reason: Epic complete: Descope auth, OrgAuth middleware, tenant isolation, multi-user dev mode, 10 E2E tests
description: Epic: Implement shared AuthZ layer that works identically in dev and descope modes. AuthN is the only difference between modes — dev validates against api_tokens table, descope validates JWTs. AuthZ checks org membership + role for every resource access. DB schema already exists (users, organization_members, api_tokens tables in 0001_initial.up.sql). This epic covers: DB-backed token auth, dev bootstrap, AuthZ service + middleware, tenant isolation, and management APIs.
title: Multi-Tenant Authorization

close_reason: Auth middleware wired: extracts Authorization header, validates via Authenticator interface, stores Caller in context. DevAuthenticator works for dev mode. Descope can swap in later.
description: Integrate Descope Go SDK for authentication. Implement middleware that extracts Authorization header, supports two schemes: 'token <api-token>' for regular API calls and 'update-token <lease-token>' for update operations. Validate tokens via Descope SDK. Map Descope user to internal user model (must populate githubLogin field). Create internal/auth/service.go and internal/http/middleware/auth.go.
title: Descope auth middleware + token validation

close_reason: Implemented DescopeAuthenticator with ExchangeAccessKey, OrgAuth middleware, multi-user dev mode, and wired into main.go
description:
Replace DevAuthenticator with Descope SDK integration. Single authenticator handles both flows:

**Access Key flow (CLI):**
1. CLI sends Authorization: token <descope-access-key>
2. Middleware strips 'token ' prefix, calls descopeClient.Auth.ExchangeAccessKey(ctx, key, nil)
3. Returns *descope.Token with tenant/role claims

**JWT flow (Web UI):**
1. Browser sends Authorization: Bearer <jwt>
2. Middleware calls descopeClient.Auth.ValidateSessionWithToken(jwt)
3. Returns *descope.Token with same tenant/role claims

**Implementation:**
- Add github.com/descope/go-sdk dependency
- Create internal/auth/descope.go implementing Authenticator interface
- Both flows produce the same Caller struct with OrgMemberships populated from token.GetTenants()
- Add role check to existing Auth middleware: extract {org} from URL, verify caller has tenant membership with sufficient role (GET→viewer, write ops→member, destructive→admin). Return 403 if denied.
- Wire in main.go: initialize Descope client with STRATA_DESCOPE_PROJECT_ID

**Dev mode:** Keep a thin DevAuthenticator that returns hardcoded Caller with OrgMemberships matching STRATA_DEV_ORG_LOGIN as admin. No DB, no tokens — just a mock of what Descope would return. This is the ONLY difference between dev and prod.

**Remove:** api_tokens table (migration), custom token hashing, token management code.
title: Implement Descope auth — Access Keys + JWTs with tenant roles
